description: Lock The Counter-Reformation to Catholic reform and doctrinal response anchored at Trent, not scientific-society organization.

checks.reason: The Counter-Reformation is defined around Catholic response to the Protestant Reformation.
The Protestant Reformation is a defining antecedent, not just generic context.
Humanist scholarship and rhetoric remain relevant to papal reform, apologetic writing, and Counter-Reformation censorship.
Humanism supplies intellectual tools without defining the movement itself.
Scholasticism is the better existing graph node for Tridentine dogmatic debate and clerical-theological education context.
Scholasticism is shared theological context, not the direct institution of the Council of Trent.
Scientific societies should not be restored unless the node is explicitly rescoped to early modern scientific organizations.
The node remains anchored to the opening of the Council of Trent.
